articles.title: The Open Source AI China Problem Just got Worse
articles.content:
嗨，我是迈克。每周我都会分享关于科技、商业、社会与未来交叉领域的AI文章。如果你想支持这个频道或获得我作品的完整访问权限，请点击这里。阅读存档 | 查看Substack笔记 | 访问我们的社区聊天 | 访问主页。

我已经追踪中美AI及其未来动态近六年了。你如何总结2026年7月AI行业的热度？这是一个非常奇怪且多层次的戏剧性时刻。我们正在见证历史。

你可能已经知道，中国公司Moonshot AI发布了其Kimi模型的新版本Kimi K3，这正在促使许多企业AI转向开源权重模型。这可能是2026年最大的AI时刻之一。随着伊朗战争在霍尔木兹海峡悬而未决，以科技股为主的纳斯达克100指数自由落体般下跌，这正成为一个地缘政治和特朗普政府的两难困境。战争和AI一样，都没有明确的解决方案。

生成式AI模型正在演进，但可能会减缓AI巨头OpenAI和Anthropic的收入增长。随着美国超大规模企业通过惊人的AI资本支出和数据中心投资走向负自由现金流，你不得不怀疑这一切是否值得——如果中国模型能够越来越大、越来越高效，在降低成本的同时复制性能的话。这有可能在半导体繁荣似乎已经进入熊市回调之后，成为股市上的AI危机。韩国（KOSPI指数）现在是一个领先指标。随着中国DRAM制造商CXMT即将在上海上市，未来AI领域的中美对抗格局非常紧张。

DeepSeek令人震惊的融资轮次和计划中的IPO——DeepSeek、Moonshot、OpenAI等都将在2027年进行——预示着明年也将是相当精彩的一年。DeepSeek在上个月6月融资约74亿美元。我们必须假设Databricks也是企业AI转向路由和更便宜的开放式权重玩家代币这一趋势的受益者之一，在这个背景下Databricks宣布了一轮新的融资，使该公司估值达到1880亿美元。

谷歌的Gemini 3.5 Pro在最糟糕的时刻被关键性地推迟了。SpaceXAI的新模型Grok 4.5的发布完全被Kimi K3时刻所掩盖。Anthropic Fable 5的混乱给了中国一个从2025年1月DeepSeek时刻的死灰复燃的完美机会。OpenAI自己的GPT 5.6 Sol发布也几乎完全被掩盖了。

特朗普政府迅速限制Mythos级模型面临一个严重问题：如果中国模型能够接近那些被认为还需要更多个月才能达到的能力呢？特朗普政府显示出可能禁止中国尖端模型并采取严厉措施遏制中国AI崛起的迹象。我以为他们不会监管AI行业。全球自由市场资本主义就这样了。

微软、亚马逊、Meta甚至谷歌等七大超大规模企业在这轮周期中失去了一些AI话语权和可信度。政府对Mythos级模型的处理不当以及Kimi K3类模型的崛起正形成一场完美风暴，导致许多企业转向更理性、更具成本效益的代币使用方式。

虽然Open-Router不能代表整个情况，但它是一个有趣的宏观趋势数据采样点：看起来更便宜的中国开源权重模型正在赢得市场份额。在我看来，美国一些最好的开源权重模型包括Thinking Machine的Inkling（约一周前发布）以及Reflection可能会推出的任何产品。Nvidia的Nemotron是Meta之前领导地位的常被引用的替代方案。问题在于美国在开源AI方面缺乏真正的领导力。对美国来说，这可能在模型、代币和企业采用的前沿领域造成AI领导力的潜在灾难。

更奇特的是，中国国家主席习近平关于人工智能最重要的近期讲话是通过在世界人工智能大会上发表主题演讲传达的。习近平主席出席上海WAIC 2026开幕式并发表题为"携手构建公平公正的全球AI治理体系"的主题演讲。看起来中国在AI治理和监管领导力方面比美国更先进，正积极围绕全球合作主题构建。阿里巴巴自己的Qwen 3.8 Max（预览版）也将有开源权重组件，并制定了激进的国际代币定价计划。Kimi K3的目标不是业余开发者，而是企业客户，以在约六个月后IPO之前提高年度经常性收入。

中美在模型领域的竞争，即使在优秀的应用层产品寥寥无几的时刻，也夸大了算力需求，而中国拥有更便宜的代币生成和更充足的能源。在这个时刻，特朗普政府的核心任务似乎是让AI繁荣在金融精英和商业阶层眼中持续下去。与此同时，从Anthropic到Moonshot AI，每个人都在为自己最大化IPO热潮和收入销售势头做准备。

如果你参考Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index并按智能程度排名，排名大致如下：

巅峰模型性能的状况大致如下：Anthropic – Claude Fable 5、OpenAI – GPT 5.6 Sol、Moonshot AI – Kimi K3（开源权重*）、SpaceXAI – Grok 4.5、智谱 (Z.ai) – GLM 5.2（开源权重）。这些列表和基准测试模型训练表现相当人为，可能下周就会改变，下个月肯定会改变。中国的开源权重模型一直更便宜，但在2026年它们正变得更加有能力。这意味着美国公司开始采用它们来完成日常任务，以减少因Fable 5和Mythos 5级定价而失控的代币预算。如果每个新发布的中国模型都有潜力在智能方面进入前五名，这对美国的AI工业复合体来说是一个大问题。美国的AI问题不仅存在，而且在恶化。

我对DeepSeek、Moonshot AI和智谱AI的创始人怀有极大的敬意，因为你感觉到他们有真正的理想主义，而不仅仅是惊人的AI人才和商业智慧。他们的模型能力展示了在计算和资金限制下产生的复杂创新和令人难以置信的AI商业敏锐度。你从Meta、谷歌或微软的高管身上得不到同样的感觉，这里有一个相当明显的脱节。相对于BigTech的资本支出，情况开始看起来很糟糕。BigTech在生成式AI模型（和产品）方面的AI执行一直糟糕得惊人。

过去一周，这可能最好由OpenAI（新任）战略未来负责人Dean Ball在X上的一条病毒推文坦白承认。他声称开源权重模型本质上具有减速主义性质，并暗示一个开源权重模型主导的世界可能导致完全的AI共产主义，可能导致反乌托邦的噩梦。

很明显，封闭模型资本密集型公司对这些事态发展感到担忧，充满了反华情绪和国防论点。Anthropic的Mythos级模型虽然在纸面上看起来令人印象深刻，但我们不知道它们最先进的能力，因为它们已被限制对公众开放。美国AI未来战略家和说客之间的界限看起来相当模糊。正如企业AI价格战中代币的资本支出回报率前景相当糟糕一样。

我们所知道的美国保护主义是，对于他们在全球市场上无法竞争的，他们肯定会试图禁止并在国内限制。但企业转向开源权重模型已经是2026年的主导故事，这样做可能为美国公司设置开源AI竞赛可能为时已晚。这是2026年开源AI独特说服力的两难困境。如果生成式AI技术实际上能提供可靠的回报率，这不会成为问题，但这是一项新兴技术，很少有出色的AI产品可以利用这些令人难以置信的模型。

美国政府商务部去年考虑将多家中国AI实验室列入其"实体清单"，我确信这现在又提上了议程，因为美国公司在算力效率快速提升的世界中难以竞争，路由比以往任何时候都更重要，代币成本和为哪些任务使用哪些模型的螺旋式上升成本也是如此。美国政府限制其最佳封闭源公司Anthropic的最佳模型可能是一个历史性错误，导致这种痛苦的局面发生。Kimi K3时刻对于Anthropic自身在仅仅几个月前的高调AI IPO之后的喜剧性错误来说更为戏剧性，Semianalysis出版物分析了Anthropic令人难以置信的营业收入。你必须假设转向开源权重模型和政府对Mythos的劫持已经减缓了美国在AI领域的顶级公司。Semianalysis认为Anthropic预计到2026年第三季度将实现超过10亿美元的GAAP EBIT（利润率约6%），使其成为首批实现持续季度盈利的主要前沿AI实验室之一。

所有这些近期事态发展将OpenAI、Meta和SpaceXAI定位为最大的输家之一。即使Meta的Muse Spark 1.1并不是一个糟糕的模型。如果特朗普政府限制中国开源权重模型，Nvidia、Thinking Machines、Meta和Reflection（尚未发布模型）可能成为美国企业开源权重代币解决方案的大赢家。2026年中期宏观AI叙事变得更加有趣。开源AI中的定制化水平正在升级。代币效率和路由变得更加重要。AI资本支出在市场上正受到越来越多的审查。开源权重模型AI霸权不是AI共产主义或核威胁，更便宜的代币有利于杰文斯悖论以及公司、开发者和消费者如何利用AI。

整个生成式AI模型训练范式在过去五年中一直是全球语言数据的多次蒸馏劫持。对算力的需求不会因为企业选择更便宜的模型而放缓，它实际上会加速。这是整个宏观AI局势的两难困境。如果资本和垄断资本主义是美国的护城河，那么美国就是一个陷入困境的国家。AI未来将需要新型创新，而不仅仅是更好的风险投资系统。

显然，这里存在一些叙事滥用，商业整合和地缘政治会像往常一样自行解决。随着DeepSeek筹集更多资金然后匆忙上市，DeepSeek时刻不断积累。他们的旗舰模型DeepSeek-R2从未发布，这引发了对中国未来最先进模型的质疑。梁文锋拥有DeepSeek 78%到84%的股份，他们还在开发自己的芯片。

一个Anthropic和Moonshot AI都受计算能力限制的世界，谷歌再次成为LLM落后者，Meta和SpaceXAI仍在边缘。一个OpenAI的IPO看起来越来越不吸引人的世界。一个我们厌倦了等待Databricks、Crusoe、Anduril、Stripe和其他公司的IPO的时刻，而中国物理AI、AI芯片和存储器巨头正急于上市。据说中国芯片制造商CXMT公司的86亿美元首次公开募股被机构投资者超额认购超过500倍。



我预测2027年将出现机器人版的ChatGPT时刻（现在也称为Physical AI），因为有传言称Anthropic正在洽谈收购Physical Intelligence。即便如此，AI先驱Yann LeCun仍警告说，这些人形机器人在相当长的时间内都将能力不足。硅谷和华尔街迫切需要新的叙事来支撑AI繁荣的股市，他们可能再次夸大事实。目前美国市场上几乎没有纯机器人或机器人软件公司的上市公司。中国正从史诗级房地产崩盘中艰难复苏，似乎在IPO竞赛中处于领先地位。尽管Moonshot AI、智谱AI、MiniMax或较小的中国AI实验室令人印象深刻，但真正让我着迷的是开源与闭源辩论的宏观动能。在很多方面，这是一场资本与人才的较量。“这个未来在我看来是反乌托邦的地狱，但我从未遇到过一位开源模型倡导者最终不承认这就是最终结局，”来自OpenAI的Dean Ball讽刺地说道。随着本周大型科技公司财报即将发布，我们将得到更多答案。资本支出与投资回报的故事从未如此严峻。任何执行上的失败都将受到市场惩罚，最近几周我们看到一些成长股估值出现调整，SpaceX下跌25.5%，Grok 4.5再次未能达到足以改变局面的水平（标准很高）。在收购Cursor这一昂贵交易的同时，SpaceX AI似乎仍将被归为AI领域的失败者。Moonshot AI于7月16日发布Kimi K3，这是一个拥有2.8万亿参数的庞然大物，在基准测试中与Claude和GPT等美国顶级系统不相上下，并计划于7月27日完全开源权重以实现低成本自托管。到2027年，我们将知道Anthropic的IPO有多强劲——其软件技术史上最快的ARR增长正受到宏观AI逆风和政府干预的挑战。美国保护主义会保护它吗？还是市场会选出赢家？这种紧张局势将蔓延到机器人竞赛中，其涵盖范围包括太空、AI和国防，规模前所未有。如果Anthropic真的收购Physical Intelligence，我多次提到的机器人GPT时刻可能即将到来。中国Uniree的IPO已经是Physical AI未来的重要时刻。它将融资42亿元人民币（6.194亿美元），同时近年来已将入门级四足机器人价格大幅削减超过90%。由于中国天生具有机器人优势，美国正在疯狂追赶这场AI竞赛中的这一环节，2026年该领域出现了多项巨额VC融资轮。美国在世界模型和软件大脑初创公司方面似乎处于领先地位，这些公司对提高机器人实用性至关重要，而中国则拥有更多人形态机器人初创公司，以及更多无人机和新型机器人硬件形态的实验。Unitree预计将于本月晚些时候或大约在Anthropic关键IPO前三个月上市。Unitree正在迅速成为全球AI研究人员和机器人实验室的事实标准硬件平台，人形机器人竞赛可能迅速升温，特斯拉将不得不在不久的将来与SpaceX合并。我心中认为这可能在2028年初发生。如果生成式AI繁荣从高峰跌落（正如许多分析师预期的那样），我认为我们可以期待机器人竞赛取代它成为美国科技的新叙事。最终，中美AI竞争对全球开发者、消费者和企业都有好处，能带来更多实际应用。生成式AI模型或机器人能否很快带来多少现实世界价值，与投资相比又是另一个问题。初创公司加速的时间表和大幅提高的融资轮次值得注意，即使AI业务采用的紧迫性在增加。热度正在升温，2027年将变得更糟，一场为2030年代技术准备的激烈竞争正在形成。中国的能源充裕和美国的半导体霸权让局势更加有趣。
articles.summary:
👋 Hey there, I’m Mike. Each week I share AI articles at the intersection of tech, business, society and the future. If you want to support the channel or gain full-access to my work, go here. Read Archives | See Substack Notes | Visit our community Chat | Visit Homepage. I’ve been tracking the U.S. vs. China dynamics of AI and its future for nearly six years.

Get Full Access

How do you summarize the heat that is July, 2026 in the AI industry? It’s been a very bizarre and multi-layered drama. We are witnessing history.

Geopolitics and AI on the Front Burner 🔥

As you likely know, Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model called Kimi K3 that is causing a lot of Enterprise AI to switch to open-weight models. It might be one of the biggest AI moments of 2026. With the Iran war unresolved in the strait of Hormuz and the Tech heavy NASDAQ 100 in freefall, it’s becoming a geopolitical and Trump Administration catch-22. There are no clear solutions in war and AI, as it turns out.

Generative AI models are evolving, but likely slowing the revenue growth of AI behemoths OpenAI and Anthropic. With American hyperscalers approaching negative free cash flow via incredible AI capex and datacenter investments, you have to wonder whether it’s all worth it - if Chinese models that are getting larger and more efficient and can replicate the performance while under cutting the cost. It has the potential to become an AI crisis in the stock market even as the Semiconductor boom seems to have hit a bear market correction, after the U.S. listing of South Korean HBM leader, SK Hynix. South Korea (the KOSPI) is now a leading indicator.

With Chinese DRAM maker CXMT about to go public in Shanghai, it’s a very charged China vs. U.S. setup in the future of AI. With DeepSeek’s stunning funding rounds and planned IPOs for DeepSeek, Moonshot, OpenAI and others in 2027, it’s shaping up to be quite a year next year too. DeepSeek raised around $7.4 billion in June last month. We have to assume Databricks has also been one of the beneficiaries of this pivot of Enterprise AI to routing and cheaper tokens of open-weight players in a world where Databricks announced a new round of funding that values the company at $188 billion.

Google’s Gemini 3.5 Pro has been crucially delayed at the worst possible moment. The launch of SpaceXAI’s new model Grok 4.5 was completely overwhelmed by the Kimi K3 moment. Anthropic Fable 5 confusion has given China the ultimate return of that DeepSeek moment vibe back from the dead of January, 2025. OpenAI’s own GPT 5.6 Sol release has also been nearly entirely overshadowed.

The Trump Administration quick to restrict Mythos class models has a serious problem, what if Chinese models are able to approach those same capabilities with open-weight models that were thought to be many more months behind? The Trump administration is showing signs it could ban Chinese cutting-edge models and take drastic steps to try to curtail China’s rise in AI. I thought they were not going to regulate the AI industry. So much for global free market capitalism.

The Brave New World of Token Efficiency Looks Chinese

With Microsoft, Amazon, Meta and even Google behind the Big 7 hyperscalers have lost some of their AI talking points and credibility in this cycle. The mishandling of Mythos class models by the Government and the rise of Kimi K3 type models is the perfect storm that is seeing many Enterprise companies pivot to more rational token usage that’s radically more cost efficient.

While Open-Router isn’t representative of the entire situation, it’s an interesting data sampling point of the overall macro trend: it appears like cheaper open-weight models from China are winning over marketshare.

In my opinion, some of the best Open-weight models from the U.S. are Thinking Machine’s Inkling (about a week old), and whatever Reflection is likely to put out soon. Nvidia’s Nemotron is an often cited alternative to the previous leadership by Meta. The problem is the real lack of leadership in open-source AI in the United States. For America this is a potential disaster in its AI leadership on the frontier of models, tokens and Enterprise adoption.

To make matters even more peculiar, Chinese President Xi Jinping’s most significant recent remarks on artificial intelligence were delivered via a keynote address at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC). President Xi Jinping attended in Shanghai the opening ceremony of WAIC 2026 and delivered a keynote speech titled “Joining Hands to Build a Just and Equitable System for Global AI Governance.” China appears to be more advanced in AI governance and regulatory leadership than the U.S. trying to actively build global collaboration around the theme.

Alibaba’s own Qwen 3.8 Max (preview) will also have an open-weight component with an aggressive international token pricing plan. Kimi K3 is not aimed at hobbyist developers but at Enterprise customers in order to ramp ARR before they also IPO in about six months time. The U.S. and China competition in models, even at a time when there are few great application layer products is exaggerating the demand for compute at a time when China has both cheaper token generation and more abundance energy. At a time when the Trump Administration’s key mandate appears to be keeping the AI boom on the stock market rolling for the financial elite and business class. Meanwhile everyone from Anthropic to Moonshot AI are positioning themselves to maximize their IPO hype and revenue sales momentum.

Do Ranking Models even Matter Any Longer?

If you go by Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index, and rank by smartest model, the ranking is:

The state of affairs on peak model performance is roughly as follows:

  1. Anthropic – Claude Fable 5

  2. OpenAI – GPT 5.6 Sol

  3. Moonshot AI – Kimi K3 (open weights*)

  4. SpaceXAI – Grok 4.5

  5. Zhipu (Z.ai) – GLM 5.2 (open weights)

These lists and benchmarks that they models are trained to perform on are fairly artificial and likely to change next week and certainly by next month.

Compare Models

The “AI Disconnect” is Getting Worse 🔎

Chinese open-weight models have been cheaper but in 2026 they are becoming way more capable. This means U.S. companies are starting to adopt them for everyday tasks to reduce their token budgets that got out of hand with Fable 5 & Mythos 5 level pricing. If every new Chinese models has the potential to debut in the top five best models by intelligence, that’s a big problem for the AI industrial complex of the U.S. The China problem in AI for the United States isn’t just there, it’s getting worse.

I have a huge respect for the founders of DeepSeek, Moonshot AI and Zhipu AI, because you sense they have real idealism and not just incredible AI talent and business smarts. The capabilities of their models show sophisticated innovations and incredible AI business acumen given their compute and funding constraints. You don’t get the same feeling from the executives at Meta, Google or Microsoft, there’s a pretty stark disconnect. Relative to BigTech’s Capex it’s starting to look really bad. The AI execution of BigTech in Generative AI models (and product) has been surprising bad.

The past week this might have been best confessed by OpenAI’s (new) head of strategic futures Dean Ball on a viral Tweet on X. Where he said that Open-weight models are inherently decelerationist and mused that an open-weight-model-dominant world could lead to full on AI communism that could lead to a dystopian hellscape.

It’s clear the closed-model capital intensive companies are concerned about these recent developments with claims of distillation and cybersecurity concerns full of anti-china sentiment and national defense arguments. While Anthropic’s Mythos class models appear on paper impressive, we don’t know their most advanced capabilities because they have been restricted from the general public. The lines between AI future strategists and lobbyists in America looks fairly blurry. Just as the rational for Capex in the Enterprise AI price-war on tokens looks fairly poor from an ROI perspective.

Top AI Accounts to follow on LinkedIn

What we know about American protectionism is what they can’t compete with on the global market, they will definately try to ban and restrict at home. But the pivot of Enterprise companies to open-weight models has already been the dominant story of 2026, it might even be too late and by doing so they might set up an open-source AI race for U.S. companies as well in doing so. That’s the catch-22 of open-source AI’s peculiar persuasion on cost in 2026. This wouldn’t be a problem if the Generative AI technology was actually giving reliable ROI, but it’s a nascent technology and not many great AI products exist yet to leverage these incredible models.

The U.S. Administration's Commerce Department last year considered adding multiple Chinese AI labs to its "Entity List” and I’m sure that’s now again on the table as American companies struggle to compete in a world of a rapid token efficiency ramp where harnesses and routing matters more than ever and so does the spiraling costs of the tokens and which models to use for which tasks.

The U.S. Administration restricting their best closed-source company in Anthropic’s best models might have been a historic mistake that has allowed this painful situation to occur. The Kimi K3 moment is more dramatic for the comedy of errors just mere months from Anthropic’s own splashy AI IPO where Semianlaysis publication has analyzed Anthropic’s incredible operating profits. You have to assume the pivot to open-weight models and the Mythos hijacking by the Administration has slowed down America’s top company in AI. Semianalysis believe Anthropic is projected to reach over $1 billion in GAAP EBIT by Q3 2026 (a ~6% profit margin), making it one of the first major frontier AI labs to achieve sustained quarterly profitability. All of these recent events positions OpenAI, Meta and SpaceXAI as among the biggest losers. This even as Meta’s Muse Spark 1.1 isn’t such at terrible model.

What If?

If the Trump Administration restricted Chinese open-weight model Nvidia, Thinking Machines, Meta and Reflection (yet to release a model) could be among the big winners for U.S. based Open-weight token solutions for Enterprise customers.

The mid 2026 Macro AI narrative has become far more interesting. The levels of customization in open-source AI are being upgraded. Token efficiency and routing has become a bigger deal. AI Capex in the markets are coming under increased scrutiny.

A Story of Many DeepSeek Moments

Open-weight model AI Supremacy isn’t AI communism or a Nuclear threat, cheaper tokens benefits Jevons Paradox and how companies, developers and consumers can do with AI. The entire Generative AI model training paradigm has been a multi-string distillation hijack of the world’s language based data for the last five years. The demand for compute isn’t slowing because Enterprises choose cheaper models, it will actually accelerate it. That’s the catch-22 of the entire macro AI situation.

America is a troubled nation if capital and monopoly capitalism is their moat. An AI future will require new kinds of innovation and not just a better Venture Capital system. There’s obviously some narrative abuse going on here and business consolidation and geopolitics will sort itself out like usual. With DeepSeek raising more funds and then rushing to IPO, the DeepSeek moments keep piling up. Their flagship model DeepSeek-R2 was never released and it begs the question to China’s most advanced models in the future. Liang Wenfeng has a 78% to 84% stake in DeepSeek and they are also developing their own chip.

The Race to IPO in the Roaring 20s

A world where both Anthropic and Moonshot AI are compute capacity constrained, Google is an LLM laggard again, and Meta and SpaceXAI are still on the margins. A world where OpenAI’s IPO looks less appealing by the quarter. A world where we are tired of waiting for the IPOs of Databricks, Crusoe, Anduril, Stripe and others even as Chinese Physical AI, AI chip and Memory giants rush to go public. It’s claimed that Chinese chipmaker CXMT Corp's $8.6 billion initial public offering was more than 500 times oversubscribed by institutional investors.

I predict 2027 will feature elements of a ChatGPT moment for robotics (now also called Physical AI), as Anthropic is rumored to be in talks to acquire Physical Intelligence. Even as AI pioneer Yann LeCun warns of how incapable these humanoid robots are and will be for quite some time. Silicon Valley and Wall Street will be so desperate for new narratives to hold the AI boom stock market up, they might stretch the truth again.

This at a time when there’s a stark lack of pure-play robotics' or robotics-software companies even public on the U.S. market. China struggling to recover from an epic housing crash seems to be going first in the IPO race. As impressive as Moonshot AI, Zhipu, Minimax or the smaller Chinsee AI labs are, it’s the macro momentum of the Open-source vs. Closed-source debate that has me enthralled. In many ways it’s a battle of capital vs. talent.

“This future strikes me as a dystopian hellscape, but I’ve never met an open-weight models advocate who doesn’t ultimately concede this is where things end,” said Dean Ball or ironically, OpenAI.

With BigTech earnings on deck this week, we’re going to get a few more answers. The capex to ROI story has never looked so dire. Any failure to execute on Earnings is going to get punished by this market that has in recent weeks seen some valuation revisions on growth names where SpaceX is down 25.5% and Grok 4.5 was yet again not good enough to move the needle (the bar is high). With an expensive acquisition of Cursor, SpaceXAI also appears to be still regulated to be among the AI losers.

Moonshot AI launched Kimi K3 on July 16, a massive 2.8 trillion-parameter model that matches top U.S. systems like Claude and GPT on benchmarks, with plans for full open weights by July 27 to enable cheap self-hosting.

By 2027 we’ll know how strong Anthropic’s IPO could be with its fastest ARR growth in software tech history being challenged by macro AI headwinds and Government intervention. Will American protectionism shield it, or will the market pick the winners? The tension is going to spill over into a robotics race that will encompass Space, AI and National defense like never before.

GPT Moment for Robotics is Near

If Anthropic really does acquire Physical Intelligence, the GPT moment for robotics I’ve spoken about multiple times could be near. The Chinese IPO of Uniree is already a huge moment for the future of Physical AI. It would raise 4.2 billion yuan ($619.4 million) while it has aggressively drove down the price of entry-level quadruped robots by over 90% in recent times.

Since China is robotics native, this part of the AI race is something the United States is frantically trying to make up ground in with several huge VC funding rounds in 2026 in the space. The U.S. appears to have a lead in the world-model and software brain startups implicated in making robots more useful while China has way more humanoid (robots that have a human form) startups and experiments in drones and new kinds of hardware form-factors for robots.

Unitree should IPO later this month or roughly three months before the pivotal IPO of Anthropic. Unitree is rapidly becoming one of the key de facto standard hardware platforms for AI researchers and robotics labs globally with the humanoid robotics race likely to ramp up so quickly Tesla will have to merge with SpaceX in the near future. In mind this happens likely in early 2028. If the Generative AI boom falls from the heights (like many analysts expect it will) I think we can expect the robotics race to take its place as the next big American technology narrative.

Ultimately the China vs. American competition in AI is good for global developers, consumers and businesses for more real-world utility. Whether Generative AI models or robots will bring much real world value anytime soon is another question entirely compared to the investment. The accelerated timelines of startups with drastically higher funding rounds is notable even as the urgency to increase AI business adoption increases. The heat is on and it’s going to get worse in 2027 with a tremendous competition setting up for the 2030s in technology. China’s energy abundance and American semiconductor supremacy keeps things interesting.
